observations.recommendation:
Municipal Accountant to revisit the current and prior year transactions and balances of all accounts with the end in view of maintaining subsidiary ledgers as of December 31, 2023 in preparation for the CY 2024 financial statements.

Municipal Mayor to consider the hiring of additional competent personnel to complement the current manpower of the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

Municipal Accountant draws JEVs to reverse the adjustments, conduct tracing of current and prior year intra-agency transactions with the end in view of providing the accurate adjusting entries and reconciled intra-agency accounts.

Municipal Treasurer submits to the Audit Team the duly certified list of taxpayers to support the accrual of real property and special education tax payables as of December 31, 2023 totaling ₱3,647,581.06, including a written explanation on the failure to accrue such at the beginning of the year. 

Municipal Accountant submits a written explanation for the noted differences of ₱182,329.06 between the deferred real property and special education tax accounts.

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace and analyze all transactions recorded in each of the aforementioned records as far as practicable to identify the source of the noted differences; and

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer initiate steps towards the proper recording of the sources of the noted differences in the appropriate records.

Municipal Treasurer determines the total net due to DPWH as of December 31, 2023; and


Municipal Treasurer initiates steps towards the transfer of the 15 per cent share for OBO and the net due to DPWH as of December 31, 2023 to the Trust Fund Account.
 
Municipal Accountant draws JEVs to recognize the transfer of the 15 per cent share for OBO and the net due to DPWH as of December 31, 2023 to the Trust Fund Account.
Municipal Accountant prepares a JEV to correct the overstatement of the Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Fund expense account of ₱38,000.00.

Municipal Mayor requests the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a tax ordinance authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to deputize collection of community taxes to barangay treasurers pursuant to Article 250 of the IRR of RA No. 7160; and

Municipal Mayor ensures that the Municipal Treasurer implement the deputization of the collection of community taxes to barangay treasurers, upon the enactment of a valid tax ordinance authorizing such.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit a written explanation for the noted deficiencies, including the submission of other related documents, for further evaluation; and  

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials continue the implementation of the terminated infrastructure project using the options provided for in Item 9.4 of Annex E of the 2016 revised IRR of RA No. 9184, with the end in view of completing such project for the benefit of its constituents.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials initiate steps in filling-in the newly-created positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with civil service rules to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management; and

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit an explanation for the noted creation of the Municipal Government Department Head I position for the LDRRMO inconsistent with Item 5.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, including proof that the Municipality is financially capable and has not exceeded the PS limitation.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials establish efficient internal controls over financial accounting and reporting of the Municipality, with the end in view of preventing the delayed submission of CY 2024 vouchers, documents and books of accounts to the Audit Team and to prevent from being imposed with the suspension of payment of salaries in accordance with Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials confer with the BSP on the matter of the proper steps to write-off the said loan in accordance with Section 49 of the General Banking Law of 2000.

Recognize the proper RPT/SET receivables and deferred income accounts and discounts for advance and prompt payments upon collection;

Make the necessary adjustments by following the proposed adjusting journal entries in Annex E

The Disposal Committee, initiate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 162 of COA Circular 92-386;


Municipal Accountant, fast track the assessment of ascertaining the recoverable service amount of the assets, and thereafter, set up the allowance for impairment consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21, to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Prepare and submit the RPCPPE and IIRUP in the prescribed format in accordance with the templates provided in COA Circulars 2002-003.

The Inventory Committee undertake and adhere to COA Circular 2020-006 in order to arrive at a reliable PPE balance that is verifiable as to existence, conditions, and accountability.

Provide details and reconcile the noted differences in the Due to NGAs account amounting to ₱21.824 million and ₱38,148 million for GF and TF, respectively.

Retrieve/locate the MOA, Trust Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding to identify the SAs and facilitate the liquidation and return of the unused funds, if there are any; and

Reclassify the account from Due to Other Funds-TF to Due to NGAs-TF

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan for the utilizations/disbursements which were charged against the lump-sum appropriation for the project: Rehabilitation of Municipal/Barangay Road in 14 Barangays, with an appropriation of ₱8 million to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit the amended/supplemental AIP, and henceforth, ensure its approval, the same shall be attached as a supporting document upon the submission of the realignment ordinance for enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials exhaust all possible remedies to collect and demand liquidation of cash advances from accountable officers, otherwise request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

Follow up with the COA-ICFAU the status of liquidation of cash advance from confidential fund.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials require the contractor to immediately undertake the necessary measures to rectify the deficiencies in the project or a refund of the equivalent amount should be collected from the contractor, otherwise responsible persons should be held liable for the said deficiencies.

Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials require the contractor to immediately undertake the necessary measures to rectify the deficiencies otherwise a refund of the equivalent amount should be collected from the contractor.

We recommended that the municipal accountant periodically conduct reconciliation of the said accounts and provide for appropriate adjustments on the noted variances.



Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials direct the accountable officers to fully settle the remaining outstanding cash advances, otherwise, failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary and the instruction of other sanctions as provided under applicable laws.

Municipal Treasurer immediately demand from all liable persons to settle the above disallowance or withhold the salaries of liable persons still in service, otherwise, refusal to comply with the order or decision of the Commission shall constitute a ground for contempt and/or referral of the matter to the Solicitor General for the filing of an appropriate civil suit or to the Ombudsman for the filing of an appropriate administrative or criminal action against the officer or employee concerned as warranted, pursuant to the provisions set forth in the revised rules and procedures of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to reconstitute an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical inventory.
We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the Inventory Committee:
Initiate steps towards the application of the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Conduct annual periodic physical count of PPE by type; and
Render the RPCPPE to the Audit Team as required in Section 124 of the Manual.
Further, we recommended and the Supplies Office agreed to maintain Property Cards per category to properly monitor the recordings of all PPEs.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to reconcile PPE accounts with the Supplies Office’s records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Prepare and maintain the required SLs for each account and submit the subsidiary schedule of GL accounts; and
Analyze and determine the details of the GL accounts with discrepancies against the TB.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to consider the hiring of a Municipal Accountant and additional manpower for the Municipal Accounting Office to handle reconciliation and tracing of discrepancies.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Reconcile their records and adjust accordingly the RPT and SET balances in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
Record the following proposed journal entry to adjust the Due to LGUs and Deferred Tax accounts as of December 31, 2024:
Ensure consistent and proper recording of the Municipality’s share of the discounts on RPT/SET or on advance payments, tax revenue on penalties and Prior Period Adjustments on collections from previous years.
The Municipal Accounting Office already recorded in the GF and SEF books the adjustments on the set-up of RPT and SET receivables and submitted Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 2025-05-002080 and 2025-05-000022, all dated May 5, 2025.
The Audit Team appreciated the Municipal Accountant’s prompt compliance with the audit recommendations. However, despite adjustments being made in the books of accounts in CY 2025, the balances reflected in the financial statements of the affected accounts remain misstated as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider centralizing receipts and issuances of property with the GSO, in order to facilitate proper monitoring of the Municipality's properties, including its supplies and materials.
We also recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct physical count of inventory of supplies for semestral cutoffs June 30 and December 31 of every year and render the RPCI to the Audit Team as required in Section 124 of the Manual.
Further, we recommended and the GSO agreed to maintain Stock Cards and render weekly the SSMI, based on the RIS, to properly monitor the recordings of all receipts and issuances of supplies.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Use the SSMI prepared by the GSO, in preparing the JEV to record the supplies and materials issued; and
Reconcile the inventory account with GSO's records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements for all accounts to enable timely verification and communication of audit results, particularly when deficiencies are identified; and
Prepare adjusting entries to correct the CIB-LCCA balances on the reconciling items which may be identified in the bank reconciliation.
On March 21, 2025, the Accounting personnel submitted bank reconciliations statements on nine of the Municipality’s bank accounts. It showed various unrecorded reconciling items, which are still subject to adjustments in 2025.
The Audit Team appreciated the Accounting personnel’s compliance with the audit recommendations. However, further reconciliation and recording of adjustments are still to be made in the books of accounts in CY 2025.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Conduct a regular reconciliation and tracing of the Intra-agency receivable and payable accounts and transactions to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy and to be able to effect necessary adjustments to bring the balances of the accounts into agreement; and
Facilitate the transfer of the funds, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to and from the accounts to minimize the accumulation of reconciling items between the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Review and reconcile PPE accounts with discrepancies in the recorded Accumulated Depreciation and effect necessary adjustments;
Compute the depreciation of the PPE accounts in accordance with IPSAS No. 17 and its PAG, and the estimated useful life as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2003-007 may be used; and
Record the following proposed journal entries to adjust the understatement of Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2024:
The Municipal Accounting Office already recorded in the GF and SEF books the adjustments on the Accumulated Depreciation and submitted JEV Nos. 2025-01-001880, 2025-01-001881 and 2025-01-000021, all dated January 1, 2025. In addition, transfer of assets from TF to GF were also recorded under JEV Nos. 2025-01-001878 and 2025-01-000104, both dated January 1, 2025.
The Audit Team appreciated the Municipal Accountant’s prompt compliance with the audit recommendations. However, despite adjustments being made in the books of accounts in CY 2025, the balances reflected in the financial statements of the affected accounts remain misstated as of December 31, 2024.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to set-up the allowance for impairment consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21 and 27, to present fairly the Breeding Stocks account in the FS.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Disclose the nature of prior period errors in the Notes to FS, in accordance with IPSAS 3; and
Present in the SCNAE each item of revenue and expense for the period that is recognized directly in net assets/equity, and the total of these items, in accordance with IPSAS 1.
On March 28, 2025, the Accounting personnel submitted schedules of prior period errors adjustments and schedule of PPE transferred to Government Equity. It showed various unreconciled difference with the balances per FS, which are still subject for further reconciliation or adjustments in 2025.
The Audit Team appreciated the Accounting personnel's submission of the schedules. However, further reconciliation and recording of adjustments are still to be made in the books of accounts in CY 2025.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, BAC and other concerned officials agreed to submit the noted lacking supporting documents, including the original and/or certified true copies of the submitted documents already in possession of the Audit Team and the noted lacking DV, if any, for further evaluation.
The Management has submitted their reply to the previous Audit Team dated October 14, 2024 and compliance with submission of the lacking documents duly received on October 17, 2024. However, the LGU is requesting for a Certified True Copy from the Contractor and NHA since original copies of some documents were already forwarded to NHA. The copies retained by the LGU were only photocopies or scanned copies, while some documents have no copies retained.
In addition, the Management stated in their reply that the noted difference of ₱25,152,256.08 pertains to the unbilled portion of the ongoing project.
Based on the initial evaluation by the Audit Team, there are submitted documents which are not compliant and still lacks necessary information. As of May 8, 2025, no further submissions were made on the lacking documents requested by the LGU from the Contractor and NHA.

We recommended and the Municipal Accounting Office agreed to:
Reconcile, analyze and identify the cause/s of the negative balances of the Due to BIR subsidiary accounts and effect the necessary adjustments;
Ensure proper recording of taxes withheld and remittances to the SL to establish their correct balances for a fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Provide additional training to Accounting Personnel with regard to the functionalities and usage of the eNGAS software.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish efficient internal controls over financial accounting and reporting of the Municipality, such as timely submission of reports and other supporting documents to the Accounting Office in order to facilitate timely recording and reporting and to prevent the suspension of payment of salaries on the responsible officials/employees causing delay thereon pursuant to Section 122(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill for the people of the Municipality to enjoy the benefits for which this Act was created.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Assessor to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended and agreed that Management require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
Additionally, direct the Municipal Accountant to be diligent in keeping the Municipal Government's records to ensure completeness thereof.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
a. 	Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise the unrecorded real properties to its current market value and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in recording in the book of accounts; and
b. 	Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the Municipal Government.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
a. 	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her records with the Municipal Assessor, and immediately prepare the certified list of taxpayers, and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant to record the RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT/SET Receivable as at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and agreed that Municipal Treasurer prepare the list of unpaid rents as of yearend to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant for the latter to prepare Journal Voucher to recognize the appropriate Receivable and Revenue accounts. Also, strictly observe the provisions of GAM for LGUs for a fair presentation of financial statements.

7.9	We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes and institute appropriate administrative and judicial remedies, if necessary, to enforce tax compliance from delinquent taxpayers, and update the listing of real properties within the jurisdiction of the Municipality.

8.7	We recommended and agreed that Management religiously comply with all the reportorial requirements for the utilization of public funds, such as BGCM, to attain a higher degree of transparency of information and adhere to the oversight committees' full disclosure policy.

We recommended and agreed  that Management observe the rules and regulations set forth by the GPPB for the procurement of goods under the BGCM. In addition, goods procured should be within the items needed pursuant to the approved Program of Work/project design to optimally and effectively utilize the fund received from BGCM in preventing the spread of the CoVID 19 pandemic.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal voucher to recognize assets and property, plant, and equipment, and a credit to Government Equity account as follows:
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory			16,307.00
Machinery and Equipment					   86,063.50
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Equipment	17,000.00
Office Equipment					162,190.75
Other PPE						174,000.00
Government Equity					455,561.25
Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the Government Accounting Manual provisions for LGUs for the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue Inventory Custodian Slip and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for inventory and PPE items issued to prevent losses of government properties.

We recommended and agreed that Management:
a. 	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare separate books of accounts for its public utilities and other economic enterprises;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare separate cashbooks of each LEEs; and
c. 	Coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to consider the passage of an ordinance authorizing the separation of accounts of each LEE from the General Fund – Proper.

13.9   We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to apply to the Bureau of Treasury the increase of the amount of bond of the Cashier pursuant to the revised schedule of premium rates prescribed by Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to sufficiently ensure government funds from eventual losses. Additionally, the Municipal Treasurer should consider the actual cash accountability of the Cashier during the period. This should also be applied to other accountable officers to ensure government funds and properties sufficiently.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference between Cash in Bank per Subsidiary Ledgers and Cash Books as of December 31, 2019, and prepare the necessary adjustments. Moreover, instruct the accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation using the correct book balance.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the updated list of delinquent stallholders with corresponding uncollected rentals and submit to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books of accounts. Likewise, adhere to Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and follow the accrual basis in recording the succeeding income from market operations.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entry to correct the accounts affected.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Loans Payable balance per book with the records of Land Bank of the Philippines to effect adjustments, if any, to correct the accounts affected
We recommended to Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary details of the Loan Payable.

We reiterated our recommendation from the previous year audit that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the accounting unit with the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always seek prior approval from the local sanggunian for the utilization of appropriations for projects described in generic terms.

We recommended that Management will fast track the hiring of Local DRRM Officer and create the other three mandatory positions as set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO's organizational structure and manpower to carry out the DRRM plans and programs.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set under RA 10121, COA Circular 2012-002 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) for the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles. Install controls on the issuance of driver's trip tickets and issuance of fuel. Require the drivers to accomplish the trip tickets properly and to prepare the monthly report of official travels and fuel consumption reports for each vehicle.

We recommended that management collect the balance from the delinquent stallholders and impose the automatic cancellation of the contract of lease of stall in case of non-payment as provided in the revenue code.

We recommended the Management to require the Accountant to strictly comply with the provision of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG contribution government share at ₱100 per month per employee.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management prioritize the creation of the inventory committee and require the periodic physical inventory count and reconciliation of the accounting records and property records. A report thereat shall be made using the RPCPPE which shall be submitted to the auditor not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Moreover, property custodian designate shall update and renew the PAR; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with accounting ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on the program/projects/activities (PPAs) requiring the highest degree of priority in terms of urgency or impact on the four thematic areas of DRRM. Likewise, ensure efficient and effective implementation of the programmed PPAs during the year to the utilization of the fund, so that desired goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be optimally achieved in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We recommended that the Management direct the project in-charge to fast track the implementation to attain the desired objective of the projects.

We recommended strict adherence to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that Management:
	
Provide, enforce, monitor and evaluate compliance on the wearing of complete essential safety gears and equipment to all personnel that are directly involved with the collections of solid wastes of the Municipality.

Strict adherence to the requirement of Section 24 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the provision of a cover of the waste compartment of the vehicle used for the collection and transport of solid wastes to ensure its containment while in transit and to ensure the protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the guidelines in payment of BAC Honoraria under DBM Circular 2004-5A. Also, direct the Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents for further verification and to avoid suspension or disallowance in the audit.
We recommended that the Management impose legal remedies on the collection of delinquent taxes to increase/improve revenue collections.

We recommended that the Management through its GFPS to submit the GPB and GAD AR to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office to ensure that identified GAD PPAs align to the priority PPAs of the province and address gender issues and concern concerning the mandate of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management through its GFPS administer the gender analysis using the HGDG on its GAD PPAs during the preparation of the GPB as well as on the GAD AR to determine the actual expenditures that can be attributed to GAD budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards to record the details of the PPEs of the Municipality. Likewise, Property Cards should also be maintained by the Treasury Office.
